witnesses.description: Three distinct rational sample points for the basis 1, x, x^2.
Exact coefficient-to-sample replay on the same finite grid.
A small alternating residual table with equal absolute values.

checks.claim: The listed finite grid is unisolvent for quadratic polynomials.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the evaluation matrix and exact determinant.

checks.claim: The listed quadratic coefficients produce the listed finite sample values.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the matrix-vector product from coefficients to sample values.

checks.claim: The listed residual alternates signs with equal absolute value on the finite grid.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes residual values, signs, and common absolute error.

checks.claim: The duplicate-node grid is unisolvent for quadratic polynomials.
checks.notes: The validator rejects the grid because the determinant is zero and x - x^2 vanishes on all listed sample points.

checks.claim: General Chebyshev-system and minimax-approximation theorems require future Lean resources.

data.target_theorem_shape: Haar spaces, Chebyshev systems, minimax approximation, alternation theorems, compactness arguments, and infinite-dimensional functional analysis
data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependencies

checks.notes: The finite rows are checked; general Chebyshev-system theory remains a proof-assistant horizon.
